alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the precise learning/planning under uncertainty claim and provides validation, but it omits the strong quantitative claim ('orders of magnitude' improvement), indicating a tempered/revised efficiency claim and a narrower empirical framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solutions simplify the process of defining a target distribution from a goal state, making it more straightforward than defining a temporally informed target distribution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel method inspired by the Z-learning algorithm effectively learns time-constrained agent preferences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conceptual claim about target distributions into an operational result about learning time-constrained preferences; this changes the emphasis and narrows/updates the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed methods is demonstrated through simulations in standard grid-world tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of the proposed algorithms is validated through grid-world simulations, demonstrating precise model learning and planning under uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert validation via grid-world simulations and demonstration of effectiveness; the publication adds detail about what was demonstrated but does not change the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: These algorithmic advances create new opportunities for applications in neuroscience and machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution about application opportunities does not appear in the preprint claims and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method detects river sandbank regions for sand mining using multi-spectral images without any labeled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method detects river sandbank regions for sand mining using multi-spectral images in an unsupervised manner without any labeled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same unsupervised/no-labeled-data contribution with slightly clearer wording ('unsupervised'), preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distinguishing features of river sandbank regions include association with a river stream and the abundance of minerals. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The distinguishing features of river sandbank regions include proximity to river channels and unique mineral composition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the characterization language (proximity vs association, unique composition vs abundance) without changing the underlying claim about distinctive features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed technique achieves average accuracy, precision, and recall of 90.75%, 85.47%, and 73.5%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The technique achieves average accuracy, precision, and recall of 90.75%, 85.47%, and 73.5%, respectively, over the seasons from Landsat-8 images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the dataset/temporal context (seasons from Landsat-8) to the same reported performance metrics, clarifying the evaluation setting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method follows a two-step approach to detect and segregate potential high mineral regions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed work offers a cost-effective, scalable solution to detect sandbanks in dynamic riverine environments by eliminating dependence on annotations or multi-modal inputs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from a two-step methodological description to broader claims about cost-effectiveness and scalability, strengthening the contribution language and widening applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Detection Transformer (DETR) has revolutionized the design of CNN-based object detection systems, showcasing impressive performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Detection Transformer (DETR) has revolutionized the design of CNN-based object detection systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert DETR's transformative role; the publication omits the adjective 'impressive performance' but keeps the core claim identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STEMD enhances the DETR-like paradigm for multi-frame 3D object detection by addressing inter-object spatial interaction and complex temporal dependencies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STEMD enhances the DETR-like paradigm for multi-frame 3D object detection by addressing inter-object spatial interaction and complex temporal dependencies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of the output of the previous frame to initialize the query input of the decoder solves the problem of missing hard cases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Augmenting the prompts of GPT 3.5 Turbo with fix category labels significantly enhances its capability in generating fixes for flaky tests. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim away from explicit minimization of energy/delay toward a broader statement about addressing applications, changing the asserted benefit and broadening focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The task-splitting problem (TSP) is modeled as an ordinal potential game, and a decentralized algorithm is proposed to obtain its Nash equilibrium. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed decentralized algorithm for the task-splitting problem achieves Nash equilibrium in a COIN environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the proposed algorithm as achieving Nash equilibrium (a clearer result statement) while preserving the modeling and algorithmic content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The task-offloading problem (TOP) is modeled as a Markov decision process, and a double deep Q-network (DDQN) is proposed to determine the optimal offloading policy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The double deep Q-network (DDQN) model proposed for the task-offloading problem optimally determines offloading policies in the COIN context. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the DDQN contribution as an achieved optimal policy in the COIN setting, clarifying it as an outcome rather than changing substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model approach allows the COIN agent to update its policies and make more informed decisions, resulting in improved performance over time compared to traditional baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulation results demonstrate that the COIN agent's ability to update its policies leads to improved performance over traditional baseline methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents simulation evidence of the same performance-improvement claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CASSLE improves over various SSL methods, reaching state-of-the-art performance in multiple downstream tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CASSLE improves over various SSL methods, reaching state-of-the-art performance in multiple downstream tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is repeated unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method modifies the projector network to foster sensitivity to characteristics affected by augmentations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method modifies the projector network to foster sensitivity to characteristics affected by data augmentations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the word "data" to clarify the augmentation type but retains the same contribution; this is a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CASSLE is directly applicable to typical joint-embedding SSL methods regardless of their objective functions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CASSLE is directly applicable to typical joint-embedding SSL methods regardless of their objective functions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The applicability claim remains the same; only the label shifts from conclusion to contribution, not the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature extractor learns to preserve augmentation information in its representations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature extractor learns to preserve augmentation information in its representations to take advantage of auxiliary conditioning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication augments the result with an explicit purpose (auxiliary conditioning), clarifying the implication and slightly broadening the claimed utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques provided substantial performance in predicting and completing missing data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques provided substantial performance in predicting and completing missing data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result claim without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of these techniques varied based on the specific dataset and missing data pattern. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of these techniques varied based on the specific dataset and missing data pattern. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensemble models that made use of the ECOC framework significantly improved prediction accuracy and robustness compared to solo models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed privacy-preserving PGFL algorithm converges to the optimal cluster-specific solution for each cluster in linear time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm ensures local differential privacy for all clients in terms of zero-concentrated differential privacy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Measures based on the hierarchical confusion matrix were used in three real-world hierarchical classification applications and compared to established evaluation measures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports application and empirical comparison in three real-world cases, which was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed ML models struggle to maintain efficacy across different network environments due to the generalizability problem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the negative diagnostic claim about existing models struggling; it focuses on the proposed pipeline and results instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current techniques for improving dataset curation are often ill-suited or counterproductive in the network security domain. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The critical assessment of existing curation techniques in the preprint is not present in the publication, which emphasizes the proposed approach instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed augmented ML pipeline uses explainable ML tools to guide network data collection in an iterative manner. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed closed-loop ML pipeline leverages explainable ML tools to guide network data collection in an iterative fashion. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (closed-loop/augmented pipeline using explainability for iterative data collection) with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The netUnicorn platform simplifies data collection for various learning problems from diverse network environments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data-collection platform, netUnicorn, simplifies collecting data for different learning problems from multiple network environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same platform capability (netUnicorn simplifies multi-environment data collection); wording and classification (contribution/result) differ slightly but content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Iterative data collection improves a model's generalizability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed iterative data collection improves a model's generalizability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result directly, preserving the claim's content and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new datasets should be endogenously collected in the iterative process to improve model generalizability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes an explicit recommendation about endogenous dataset collection in the iterative loop, which was not explicitly asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed bi-encoder-based detectors outperform other methods for out-of-distribution detection in NLP across all datasets evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed bi-encoder-based detectors outperform other methods for out-of-distribution detection in NLP across all datasets evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simplicity of the training process and superior detection performance of the proposed method make it applicable to real-world scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simplicity of the training process of the bi-encoder-based detectors makes them applicable to real-world scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes applicability due to simplicity and separates performance (stated elsewhere), clarifying rather than contradicting the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benchmarking metrics presented in this study serve as a valuable resource for future research in out-of-distribution detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benchmarking metrics presented in this study serve as a valuable resource for future research in out-of-distribution detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this contribution statement without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed probabilistic-based feature embedding (PFE) effectively captures spatial-angular information for 4-D light field processing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed probabilistic-based feature embedding (PFE) effectively captures spatial-angular information by assembling various low-dimensional convolution patterns in a probability space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives more detail on how PFE captures spatial-angular information (assembling convolution patterns), clarifying the mechanism without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cycle-consistent 4-D LF reconstruction network constructed from coded measurements improves performance in 4-D light field tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cycle-consistent 4-D LF reconstruction network constructed from coded measurements demonstrates significant superiority over state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result but with stronger wording (significant superiority), amplifying the strength of the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating PFE into an iterative optimization framework enhances 4-D LF denoising. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating PFE into an iterative optimization framework improves 4-D LF denoising performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the direction of the claim but reports it with reduced certainty (medium vs high), tempering confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of a variational auto-encoder (VAE) allows for the simultaneous optimization of an asynchronous machine and a permanent magnet synchronous machine. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of a variational auto-encoder (VAE) allows for the simultaneous optimization of an asynchronous machine and a permanent magnet synchronous machine. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same as the preprint claim with no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate concurrent parametric multi-objective technology optimization in the high-dimensional design space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate concurrent parametric multi-objective technology optimization in the high-dimensional design space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result statement is identical between preprint and publication; no change in wording or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VAE-based approach is quantitatively compared to a classical deep learning-based direct approach for key performance indicators (KPIs) prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VAE-based approach is quantitatively compared to a classical deep learning-based direct approach for key performance indicators (KPIs) prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative claim and its level of certainty remain the same in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-Calib is a novel, fast, robust, and accurate calibration toolbox for event cameras. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-Calib is a novel, fast, robust, and accurate calibration toolbox for event cameras that utilizes the asymmetric circle grid. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the original contribution claim by specifying the use of an asymmetric circle grid, adding detail without changing the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes the asymmetric circle grid, which enhances robustness to out-of-focus scenes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-Calib is a novel, fast, robust, and accurate calibration toolbox for event cameras that utilizes the asymmetric circle grid. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication incorporates this methodological detail into the stated contribution; the content and intent are preserved, though it is merged into the contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-Calib outperforms the state-of-the-art in detection success rate, reprojection error, and estimation accuracy of extrinsic parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art in detection success rate, reprojection error, and pose estimation accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published phrasing slightly rewords 'estimation accuracy of extrinsic parameters' as 'pose estimation accuracy' without changing the substantive performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models have achieved breakthroughs on a wide range of benchmarks in natural language processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models have achieved breakthroughs on a wide range of benchmarks in natural language processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim with unchanged scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discourse should be regarded as the core of engineering design processes and represented in a digital artifact. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discourse should be regarded as the core of engineering design processes and represented in a digital artifact. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical in publication and preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Foundation models such as large language models could contribute to the design discourse by automating parts that involve creativity and reasoning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Foundation models such as large language models could contribute to the design discourse by automating parts that involve creativity and reasoning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between versions; claim preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations, experiments, topology optimizations, and other process steps can be integrated into a machine-actionable, discourse-centric design process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations, experiments, topology optimizations, and other process steps can be integrated into a machine-actionable, discourse-centric design process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this integrative claim without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research will be necessary for the implementation of the conceptualized framework. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future research will be necessary for the implementation of the conceptualized framework. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Flat minima methods can improve the performance of GNN models by over 2 points, if the train-test split is randomized. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Flat minima methods can improve the performance of GNN models by over 2 points, if the train-test split is randomized. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Randomized splits are essential for a fair evaluation of GNNs, as other (fixed) splits like 'Planetoid' are biased. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Randomized splits are essential for a fair evaluation of GNNs, as other (fixed) splits like 'Planetoid' are biased. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about split bias is unchanged in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weight averaging techniques, in particular EWA, are recommended when using early stopping for GNNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weight averaging techniques, in particular EWA, are recommended when using early stopping for GNNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Recommendation wording and emphasis are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weight averaging techniques are less sensitive to hyperparameters and need no additional training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weight averaging techniques are less sensitive to hyperparameters and need no additional training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about robustness and training cost is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automating the end-to-end benchmarking flow for machine learning models on microcontrollers is of high relevance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automating the end-to-end benchmarking flow for machine learning models on microcontrollers is of high relevance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the relevance of automation is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool MLonMCU is proposed for benchmarking TinyML frameworks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool MLonMCU is proposed for benchmarking TinyML frameworks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim introducing MLonMCU is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MLonMCU demonstrates the ability to benchmark TFLite for Microcontrollers and TVM with a large number of configurations in a low amount of time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLonMCU demonstrates the ability to benchmark TFLite for Microcontrollers and TVM with a large number of configurations in a low amount of time. (certainty=high, type=result)
